chunk_summary: Guidelines for initiating gastric feeding via bolus or continuous delivery, with specific numeric targets for infants, children, and preterm infants, including advancement strategies and precautions to minimize risks like aspiration and emesis.
learning_objectives: Understand the numeric targets for initiating and advancing gastric bolus and continuous feeding in infants and children.
Identify appropriate feeding methods and precautions for reflux-prone or aspiration-risk patients.
Learn specific feeding recommendations for preterm and very low birth weight (VLBW) infants, including tube preferences and advancement rates.
key_facts: Bolus feeding initiation: ~10–15 mL/kg per feed every 2–3 hours for infants (0–12 months), ~5–10 mL/kg per feed every 2–3 hours for children (1–6 years).
Continuous feeding initiation: ~1–2 mL/kg/hour in infants, ~1 mL/kg/hour in older children, with advancement by ~0.5–1 mL/kg/hour every 6–24 hours.
Preterm VLBW infants: Daily feed volume advancement of ~18–40 mL/kg/day is recommended.
OG tubes are preferred over NG tubes for preterm infants in Indian guidance.
Routine pre-feed abdominal girth measurement or gastric aspiration is discouraged unless other intolerance signs are present.
Small green residuals (<2–3 mL) alone should not delay feed advancement unless accompanied by additional red flags.
reference_quote: Stable preterm VLBW infants may advance daily feed volumes roughly ~18–40 mL/kg/day, and routine pre-feed abdominal girth measurement or routine gastric aspiration is discouraged in the absence of other intolerance signs.
document_key_topics: Gastric bolus feeding initiation and advancement
Feeding strategies for preterm and VLBW infants
Precautions to prevent aspiration and emesis
